title: Cultural Heritage and Climate in South-Eastern Europe and the Mediterranean: A Common Agenda in the Making
content: On May 15th and 16th, 2024, the ARCHE project convened a workshop in Ljubljana, Slovenia, bringing together high-level representatives to strengthen regional cooperation in cultural heritage. Hosted by the Slovenian Ministry of Culture, the event gathered speakers from Slovenia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Kosovo, Serbia, Montenegro, and Egypt, highlighting a growing commitment to aligning national strategies with a broader European agenda. The discussions focused on fostering cross-border cooperation and developing sustainable conservation practices to contribute to the future European Partnership for Resilient Cultura
creator: SShangyun
sourceDescription: A platform on and for cultural heritage research

title: New European Bauhaus Prizes 2026: applications are open
content: The New European Bauhaus (NEB) Prizes celebrate innovative projects and ideas that embody NEB values—sustainability, inclusivity, and beauty. Over five editions, the competition has attracted more than 5,000 applications and awarded EUR 1.54 million to 72 winners. The Prizes inspire regions, cities, and towns to embrace NEB principles, foster youth participation, and promote intergenerational dialogue. A total of 13 winners will be awarded in 2026, including two public vote prizes, one ‘NEB Water Resilience’ special prize and two ‘NEB International prizes’ for Japan and Brazil. additionally, 14 runners-up will be recognised under the Prizes. Applications
creator: SShangyun
sourceDescription: A platform on and for cultural heritage research
